'''Adega''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="FF53" /> located in the [[Auril sector/Legends|Auril sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was the source of the earliest [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystals]], known as [[Adegan crystal/Legends|Adegan crystals]],<ref name="TEA"/> and was also [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to venomous creatures known as [[Adegan eel]]s.<ref name="Crossword" />

==Description==
[[File:Adega-FF139.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Adega and its surroundings]]
Adega was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="FF53" /> located within the [[Adega system/Legends|Adega system]],<ref name="SWR" /> one of the [[Auril systems]],<ref name="CSWE-Adega system" /> and a part of the [[Auril sector/Legends|Auril sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="FF53" /> It orbited the [[Binary star system/Legends|twin]] [[Star/Legends|stars]] [[Adega Prime]] and [[Adega Besh]]. Adega was situated near the planet [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] and the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
Adega was located in the Adega system, which was once the site of an important ancient [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] stronghold.<ref name="SWR" /> Before the discovery of the planet [[Ilum/Legends|Ilum]] around [[22,800 BBY]], the earliest [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystals]] came from Adega.<ref name="TEA"/> The construction of the first [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] would have been impossible without the use of [[Adegan crystal/Legends|Adegan crystals]].<ref name="FF53" /> Even after Ilum succeeded Adega as the primary source of lightsaber [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]], the three most common varieties—[[Kathracite crystal/Legends|kathracite]], [[Mephite crystal/Legends|mephite]], and [[Pontite crystal/Legends|pontite]]—were still referred to as Adegan crystals.<ref name="TEA"/>

By [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Adega was undecided on its affiliation to either the Republic or the Confederacy.<ref name="FF139">{{FactFile|139|WOR 1-2|Worlds of the Clone Wars}}</ref>

==Inhabitants==
Adega was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the slithery [[Fish/Legends|fish]] known as [[Adegan eel]],<ref name="Crossword" /> a venomous creature with vicious [[Tooth/Legends|fangs]] and a [[Slime/Legends|slime]]-covered body.<ref name="CSWE-Adegan eel">{{CSWECite|I|11|Adegan eel}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Adega was introduced in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']], which provided the planet's only visual depiction—an image also used to illustrate twelve other worlds in the game.<ref name="SWR" /> ''Star Wars: Rebellion'' and its accompanying [[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] placed Adega in the [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]].<ref name="SWR" /><ref name="SWR Prima">[[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Rebellion'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref>

The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Adega [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square R-6. ''The Essential Atlas'' described Adega as being a "world near Ossus" in its entry for Ilum. However, the reference book did not list Adega among the planets of the Adega system in its entry for Ossus.<ref name="TEA"/> [[Jason Fry]], author of ''The Essential Atlas'', confirmed the placement of Adega in the Adega system but was not able to provide an explanation for the discrepancy.<ref name="Fry">{{EmailCite|email=File:EAOC_discussion_2025.jpg|creator=[[Jason Fry]]|date=2025-09-17}}</ref>
